01-01.name: New Year's Day
01-01.description: Observed globally on the first day of the Gregorian calendar, New Year's Day marks the start of a new year and is one of the most widely celebrated public holidays in the world.

01-27.name: International Holocaust Remembrance Day
01-27.description: Designated by the United Nations in 2005 to coincide with the liberation of Auschwitz-Birkenau, this day honours the memory of the six million Jewish victims and millions of others murdered by the Nazi regime.

03-08.name: International Women's Day
03-08.description: Celebrated since the early twentieth century and adopted by the United Nations in 1977, International Women's Day recognises women's social, economic, cultural, and political achievements while calling for gender equality worldwide.

04-22.name: Earth Day
04-22.description: First held in 1970 following the Santa Barbara oil spill, Earth Day mobilises over a billion people each year to take action on environmental protection, climate change, and biodiversity loss.

05-01.name: International Workers' Day
05-01.description: Observed in over 160 countries, International Workers' Day commemorates the 1886 Haymarket affair in Chicago and celebrates the global labour movement's historic struggles for fair wages, safe conditions, and workers' rights.

05-03.name: World Press Freedom Day
05-03.description: Proclaimed by the UN General Assembly in 1993 on the anniversary of the Windhoek Declaration, World Press Freedom Day affirms that a free press is essential to democracy and evaluates the state of press freedom worldwide.

06-05.name: World Environment Day
06-05.description: Established by the United Nations in 1972 and observed in over 143 countries, World Environment Day is the principal global platform for raising awareness and driving action on urgent environmental issues.

06-08.name: World Oceans Day
06-08.description: Officially recognised by the United Nations in 2008, World Oceans Day highlights the ocean's critical role in sustaining life on Earth and promotes global action to protect marine ecosystems from pollution and overexploitation.

06-20.name: World Refugee Day
06-20.description: Designated by the UN General Assembly in 2001 to coincide with the 50th anniversary of the 1951 Refugee Convention, World Refugee Day honours the courage and resilience of the more than 100 million forcibly displaced people worldwide.

07-18.name: Nelson Mandela International Day
07-18.description: Proclaimed by the United Nations in 2009 on the birthday of Nelson Mandela, this day invites individuals to devote 67 minutes of service to their communities — one minute for each year Mandela spent in public life.

08-19.name: World Humanitarian Day
08-19.description: Established by the UN General Assembly to mark the 2003 Baghdad bombing that killed 22 aid workers, World Humanitarian Day pays tribute to humanitarian workers killed or injured in the line of duty and calls for the protection of civilians in conflict.

09-21.name: International Day of Peace
09-21.description: Established by the United Nations in 1981 and observed with a global ceasefire since 2001, the International Day of Peace invites all nations and peoples to commit to peace above all differences and to contribute to building a culture of peace.

10-10.name: World Mental Health Day
10-10.description: Observed since 1992 under the World Federation for Mental Health and endorsed by the World Health Organisation, World Mental Health Day promotes global awareness of mental health issues and advocates for open discussion and improved access to care.

10-18.name: World Menopause Day
10-18.description: Designated by the International Menopause Society and supported by the World Health Organisation, World Menopause Day raises awareness of the menopause and the support options available for improving health and wellbeing for the over one billion women worldwide who will reach this stage of life by 2025.

11-11.name: Remembrance Day
11-11.description: Observed each year at the eleventh hour of the eleventh day of the eleventh month — the moment the Armistice ending the First World War took effect in 1918 — Remembrance Day honours the sacrifice of military personnel who have died in all wars since.

12-01.name: World AIDS Day
12-01.description: The first ever global health day when it launched in 1988, World AIDS Day calls on individuals, communities, and governments to unite in the fight against HIV, remember those who have died, and support the more than 38 million people living with the virus.

12-10.name: Human Rights Day
12-10.description: Observed on the anniversary of the UN General Assembly's adoption of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 1948, Human Rights Day reaffirms the inalienable rights and fundamental freedoms to which every person on Earth is entitled.
